title: Bootstrapped Validation of Model Performance and Robustness
text:
1 Load Project Resources


Code
# clear environment
rm(list = ls(envir = .GlobalEnv), envir = .GlobalEnv)
invisible(gc(full = TRUE))
invisible(lapply(grep(":|autoloads|globalenv", search(),
  invert = TRUE, value = TRUE, ignore.case = TRUE
), detach, character.only = TRUE, unload = TRUE, force = TRUE))

# source custom functions (consistent with steps 7-11)
purrr::walk(file.path(here::here("assets", "functions"), paste0(c(
  "set_globals", "import_pipes", "numeric_df", "add_style",
  "md_to_html", "rm_dup_cols", "format_coef", "suppress_output",
  "named_list", "sci_to_tex", "as_kable", "pval_str", "footnote",
  "beautify_names", "format_p_value", "structure_models",
  "dim_ci_rows", "row_grouping"
), ".R")), source)

# set global options and import pipe operators
set_globals()
import_pipes()

# global recompute switch
# TRUE re-runs the heavy steps; FALSE loads cached assets when they exist
recompute_heavy <- FALSE

title: Bootstrapped Validation of Model Performance and Robustness
text:
2 Load Data and Models


Code
# define input file paths
adjusted_data_path <- here::here("assets", "data", "saved", "adjusted_data.rds")
unadjusted_fits_path <- here::here("assets", "models", "unadjusted_fits.rds")
adjusted_fits_path <- here::here("assets", "models", "adjusted_fits.rds")

# verify input files exist
required_files <- c(adjusted_data_path, unadjusted_fits_path, adjusted_fits_path)
missing_files <- required_files[!file.exists(required_files)]
if (length(missing_files) > 0) {
  stop("Missing required input files: ", paste(basename(missing_files), collapse = ", "))
}

# load adjusted dataset from step 8
source_data <- readRDS(adjusted_data_path)

# load fits and structure into nested list (unadjusted/adjusted × ols/robust)
unadjusted_fits <- readRDS(unadjusted_fits_path)
adjusted_fits <- readRDS(adjusted_fits_path)
model_fits <- structure_models(unadjusted_fits, adjusted_fits)

# extract robust models from adjusted fits and assign custom class for dispatch
lmrobs <- model_fits$adjusted$robust %>%
  purrr::map(~ `class<-`(.x, c("model", class(.x))))

# extract model ids
model_ids <- names(lmrobs) %>%
  stringr::str_extract("[0-9]+$") %>%
  as.integer() %>%
  sort()




Code
# verification block to confirm step 7/9 alignment
n_models <- length(model_ids)
model_range <- paste0("Models ", min(model_ids), "–", max(model_ids))
n_obs <- nrow(source_data)

verify_msg <- glue(
  "<div class='callout-note'>
<strong>Step 7/9 Model Verification</strong><br><br>
<strong>Models loaded:</strong> {n_models} models ({model_range})<br>
<strong>Adjusted fits:</strong> From Step 9 (<code>adjusted_fits.rds</code>)<br>
<strong>Analysis dataset:</strong> {n_obs} observations (sensitivity sample from Step 8)<br>
<strong>Model types:</strong> Composite (Models 0–6) and Disaggregated (Models 7–11)
</div>"
)
cat(verify_msg)







Note



Step 7/9 Model Verification Models loaded: 12 models (Models 0–11) Adjusted fits: From Step 9 (adjusted_fits.rds) Analysis dataset: 1030 observations (sensitivity sample from Step 8) Model types: Composite (Models 0–6) and Disaggregated (Models 7–11)

title: Initial Model Evaluation
text:
1 Setup


Code
# clear environment
rm(list = ls(envir = .GlobalEnv), envir = .GlobalEnv)
invisible(gc(full = TRUE))
invisible(lapply(grep(":|autoloads|globalenv", search(),
  invert = TRUE, value = TRUE, ignore.case = TRUE
), detach, character.only = TRUE, unload = TRUE, force = TRUE))

# source custom functions (consistent with steps 7-9)
purrr::walk(file.path(here::here("assets", "functions"), paste0(c(
  "format_p_value", "as_kable", "named_list", "structure_models",
  "add_style", "format_coef", "pval_str", "footnote", "sci_to_tex",
  "import_pipes", "dim_ci_rows", "set_globals", "row_grouping",
  "suppress_output", "beautify_names", "md_to_html", "numeric_df"
), ".R")), source)

# set global options and import pipe operators
set_globals()
import_pipes()

# global recompute switch
# TRUE re-runs the heavy steps; FALSE loads cached assets when they exist
recompute_heavy <- FALSE




Code
# define input file paths
adjusted_data_path <- here::here("assets", "data", "saved", "adjusted_data.rds")
unadjusted_fits_path <- here::here("assets", "models", "unadjusted_fits.rds")
adjusted_fits_path <- here::here("assets", "models", "adjusted_fits.rds")

# verify input files exist
required_files <- c(adjusted_data_path, unadjusted_fits_path, adjusted_fits_path)
missing_files <- required_files[!file.exists(required_files)]
if (length(missing_files) > 0) {
  stop("Missing required input files: ", paste(basename(missing_files), collapse = ", "))
}

# define output asset paths (gated by recompute_heavy below)
fit_metrics_path <- here::here("assets", "models", "fit_metrics.rds")
nested_rlm_unadj_path <- here::here("assets", "models", "nested_rlm_unadjusted.rds")
nested_rlm_adj_path <- here::here("assets", "models", "nested_rlm_adjusted.rds")
nested_ols_unadj_path <- here::here("assets", "models", "nested_ols_unadjusted.rds")
nested_ols_adj_path <- here::here("assets", "models", "nested_ols_adjusted.rds")

# load source data from step 8 (sensitivity dataset with influential obs removed)
source_data <- readRDS(adjusted_data_path)

# load fits and structure into nested list (unadjusted/adjusted × ols/robust)
unadjusted_fits <- readRDS(unadjusted_fits_path)
adjusted_fits <- readRDS(adjusted_fits_path)
model_fits <- structure_models(unadjusted_fits, adjusted_fits)

# extract model ids from stored fits
model_ids <- names(model_fits$adjusted$robust) %>%
  stringr::str_extract("[0-9]+$") %>%
  as.integer() %>%
  sort()




Code
# verification block to confirm step 7/9 alignment
n_models <- length(model_ids)
model_range <- paste0("Models ", min(model_ids), "–", max(model_ids))
n_obs <- nrow(source_data)

cat(glue(
  "<div class='callout-note'>
<strong>Step 7/9 Model Verification</strong><br><br>
<strong>Models loaded:</strong> {n_models} models ({model_range})<br>
<strong>Unadjusted fits:</strong> From Step 7 (<code>unadjusted_fits.rds</code>)<br>
<strong>Adjusted fits:</strong> From Step 9 (<code>adjusted_fits.rds</code>)<br>
<strong>Analysis dataset:</strong> {n_obs} observations (sensitivity sample from Step 8)<br>
<strong>Model types:</strong> Composite (Models 0–6) and Disaggregated (Models 7–11)
</div>"
))

title: Initial Model Evaluation
text:
3 Model Fit Statistics


Code
# extract goodness-of-fit metrics from model object
get_metrics <- function(model_id, adjusted, robust, fits = model_fits) {
  lev_type <- ifelse(adjusted, "adjusted", "unadjusted")
  est_method <- ifelse(robust, "robust", "ols")
  model_key <- paste0("model_", model_id)

  model_fit <- fits[[lev_type]][[est_method]][[model_key]]$fit

  if (!inherits(model_fit, c("lmrob", "lm"))) {
    stop("Unsupported model class: ", class(model_fit)[1])
  }

  fit_sum <- summary(model_fit)
  n_obs <- length(fit_sum$residuals)
  n_params <- length(coef(model_fit)) + 1

  # compute information criteria (built-in for lm, approximated for lmrob)
  if (inherits(model_fit, "lm") && !inherits(model_fit, "lmrob")) {
    log_lik <- logLik(model_fit)
    aic <- AIC(model_fit)
    bic <- BIC(model_fit)
    rse_lbl <- "Residual_Std_Error"
  } else {
    # approximate log-likelihood for robust models assuming normal errors
    log_lik <- -n_obs / 2 * (log(2 * pi) + 2 * log(fit_sum$sigma) + 1)
    aic <- -2 * log_lik + 2 * n_params
    bic <- -2 * log_lik + log(n_obs) * n_params
    rse_lbl <- "Robust_RSE"
  }

  metrics <- data.frame(
    R_squared = fit_sum$r.squared,
    Adj_R_squared = fit_sum$adj.r.squared,
    RSE = fit_sum$sigma,
    AIC = aic,
    BIC = bic
  )
  colnames(metrics)[3] <- rse_lbl

  metrics
}




Code
# load cached metrics when available, otherwise compute the full grid
if (!recompute_heavy && file.exists(fit_metrics_path)) {
  metrics_df <- readRDS(fit_metrics_path)
} else {
  # create parameter grid for all model × leverage × method combinations
  params <- tidyr::expand_grid(
    model = model_ids,
    adjusted = c(TRUE, FALSE),
    robust = c(TRUE, FALSE)
  )

  # compute metrics for each combination
  metrics_df <- purrr::pmap_dfr(params, function(model, adjusted, robust) {
    met <- get_metrics(model, adjusted, robust)
    data.frame(
      model = model,
      leverage = ifelse(adjusted, "Adjusted", "Unadjusted"),
      method = ifelse(robust, "RLM", "OLS"),
      R_squared = met$R_squared,
      Adj_R_squared = met$Adj_R_squared,
      RSE = met[, 3],
      AIC = met$AIC,
      BIC = met$BIC
    )
  }) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(model, leverage) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      leverage = dplyr::if_else(dplyr::row_number() == 1,
        stringr::str_to_title(leverage), NA_character_
      )
    ) %>%
    dplyr::ungroup()

  # suppress R² for intercept-only model (model 0) as it is undefined
  r2_cols <- grepl("R_squared", names(metrics_df))
  metrics_df[metrics_df$model == 0, r2_cols] <- NA

  saveRDS(metrics_df, fit_metrics_path)
}

# create row labels for table grouping
model_rows <- paste("Model", metrics_df$model)




Code
# table column names and footnote
col_names_fits <- c(
  "Leverage", "Method", "$R^2$", "$R^2 (adj.)$",
  "Std. Error (RSE / Robust RSE)", "AIC", "BIC"
)

footnote_text_fits <- glue(
  "'Leverage' indicates whether influential observations were removed (Adjusted vs. Unadjusted).",
  "'Method' specifies estimation approach (OLS or RLM).",
  "$R^2$ denotes coefficient of determination;",
  "$R^2 (adj.)$ is its adjusted variant. 'Std. Error'",
  "reports residual standard error (OLS) or",
  "robust residual scale estimate (RLM).",
  "AIC and BIC are Akaike and Bayesian information criteria. ",
  "Higher $R^2$ indicates better fit;",
  "lower AIC/BIC suggests better model performance. ",
  "RLM AIC/BIC are normal-likelihood approximations and are not directly comparable to OLS values."
)

metrics_df %>%
  dplyr::select(-model) %>%
  as_kable(sci_threshold = FALSE, omit_leading_zero = TRUE, digits = 3) %>%
  kableExtra::pack_rows(index = row_grouping(model_rows), escape = FALSE) %>%
  footnote() %>%
  add_style()

title: Initial Model Evaluation
text:
4 Nested Model Comparisons


Code
# compare two nested models using Wald and deviance tests
compare_models <- function(mod1, mod2) {
  extract_p_value <- function(x) {
    pr_cols <- grep("^Pr", names(x), value = TRUE)
    if (length(pr_cols) == 0) {
      return(NA_real_)
    }
    tail(x[[pr_cols[1]]], 1)
  }

  tryCatch(
    {
      if (inherits(mod1, "lmrob")) {
        wald <- anova(mod1, mod2, test = "Wald")
        dev <- anova(mod1, mod2, test = "Deviance")

        data.frame(
          df1 = mod1$degree.freedom,
          df2 = mod2$degree.freedom,
          wald_stat = tail(wald$Test.Stat, 1),
          wald_p = extract_p_value(wald),
          deviance_stat = tail(dev$Test.Stat, 1),
          deviance_p = extract_p_value(dev),
          df = tail(wald$Df, 1)
        )
      } else {
        wald <- lmtest::waldtest(mod1, mod2, test = "Chisq")
        aov <- anova(mod1, mod2)

        data.frame(
          df1 = mod1$df.residual,
          df2 = mod2$df.residual,
          wald_stat = wald$Chisq[2],
          wald_p = extract_p_value(wald),
          deviance_stat = aov$F[2],
          deviance_p = extract_p_value(aov),
          df = aov$Df[2]
        )
      }
    },
    error = function(e) {
      warning("Model comparison failed: ", e$message)
      data.frame(
        df1 = NA, df2 = NA, wald_stat = NA, wald_p = NA,
        deviance_stat = NA, deviance_p = NA, df = NA
      )
    }
  )
}




Code
# generate nested model comparison table
compare_nested_models <- function(fits, leverage, method,
                                  col_names = NULL, footnote_text = NULL) {
  models <- purrr::map(fits[[leverage]][[method]], ~ .x$fit)

  mn <- names(models)
  model_nums <- as.integer(sub(".*_", "", mn))

  ord <- order(model_nums)
  models <- models[ord]
  mn <- mn[ord]
  model_nums <- model_nums[ord]

  is_nested <- function(mod1, mod2) {
    terms1 <- attr(terms(mod1), "term.labels")
    terms2 <- attr(terms(mod2), "term.labels")
    all(terms1 %in% terms2) && !setequal(terms1, terms2)
  }

  num_combs <- combn(model_nums, 2, simplify = FALSE)
  name_combs <- combn(mn, 2, simplify = FALSE)

  nested_idx <- purrr::map_lgl(name_combs, function(nv) {
    is_nested(models[[nv[1]]], models[[nv[2]]])
  })

  num_combs <- num_combs[nested_idx]
  name_combs <- name_combs[nested_idx]

  model_pairs <- Map(
    function(nums, nv) setNames(nums, nv),
    num_combs, name_combs
  )

  comps <- model_pairs %>%
    purrr::map_df(~ {
      mod1 <- models[[names(.x)[1]]]
      mod2 <- models[[names(.x)[2]]]
      data.frame(
        model_a = .x[1],
        model_b = .x[2],
        model_pair = sprintf("%d vs. %d", .x[1], .x[2]),
        suppress_output(compare_models(mod1, mod2))
      )
    })

  labs <- glue("Model {comps$model_a}")

  comps %>%
    dplyr::rowwise() %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      wald_stat = format_coef(wald_stat, wald_p),
      deviance_stat = format_coef(deviance_stat, deviance_p)
    ) %>%
    dplyr::ungroup() %>%
    dplyr::select(-model_a, -model_b, -wald_p, -deviance_p) %>%
    as_kable() %>%
    kableExtra::pack_rows(index = row_grouping(labs), escape = FALSE) %>%
    footnote() %>%
    add_style()
}



4.1 Robust Linear Models (RLM)


Code
# column names and footnote for RLM nested comparisons
col_names <- c(
  "$\\text{}$",
  "$DF_{\\text{pseudo1}}$",
  "$DF_{\\text{pseudo2}}$",
  "Wald $\\chi^2$",
  "Deviance",
  "DF"
)

footnote_text <- glue(
  "{pval_str(use_dot_for_0.1 = TRUE, html = FALSE)}. ",
  "Pseudo-degrees of freedom ($DF_{{\\text{{pseudo}}}}$)",
  "represent adjusted degrees of freedom in robust models. ",
  "$DF_{{\\text{{pseudo1}}}}$ corresponds to the simpler model;",
  "$DF_{{\\text{{pseudo2}}}}$ to the more complex model. ",
  "Wald and Deviance statistics assess improvements in nested models."
)




Code
if (!recompute_heavy && file.exists(nested_rlm_unadj_path)) {
  nested_rlm_unadjusted <- readRDS(nested_rlm_unadj_path)
} else {
  nested_rlm_unadjusted <- compare_nested_models(model_fits, "unadjusted", "robust")
  saveRDS(nested_rlm_unadjusted, nested_rlm_unadj_path)
}

title: Influence Diagnostics & Sensitivity Sample
text:
Code
# clear global environment, garbage collect, and detach packages
rm(list = ls(envir = .GlobalEnv), envir = .GlobalEnv)
invisible(gc(full = TRUE))
invisible(lapply(grep(":|autoloads|globalenv", search(),
  invert = TRUE, value = TRUE, ignore.case = TRUE
), detach, character.only = TRUE, unload = TRUE, force = TRUE))

# source custom functions (consistent with step 7)
purrr::walk(file.path(here::here("assets", "functions"), paste0(c(
  "as_kable", "set_globals", "pval_str", "add_style", "sci_to_tex",
  "md_to_html", "describe", "format_p_value", "std_res", "footnote",
  "beautify_names", "min_bootstraps", "numeric_df", "import_pipes"
), ".R")), source)

# set global options and import pipe operators
set_globals()
import_pipes()
Code
# define input paths from step 7
unadjusted_data_path <- here::here("assets", "data", "saved", "unadjusted_data.rds")
unadjusted_fits_path <- here::here("assets", "models", "unadjusted_fits.rds")

# validate input files exist
if (!file.exists(unadjusted_data_path)) {
  stop("Missing input from Step 7: ", unadjusted_data_path)
}
if (!file.exists(unadjusted_fits_path)) {
  stop("Missing input from Step 7: ", unadjusted_fits_path)
}

# load unadjusted dataset
unadjusted_data <- readRDS(unadjusted_data_path)
if (!is.data.frame(unadjusted_data)) {
  stop("'unadjusted_data' must be a data.frame.")
}

# retrieve robust regression models from step 7
# step 7's robust_analysis() returns structured_models objects:
# - $robust_model_results$model_object for lmrob fits
# - $ols_model_results$model_object for OLS fits
fits_raw <- readRDS(unadjusted_fits_path)
if (!is.list(fits_raw) || length(fits_raw) < 2) {
  stop("Expected a list of model fits in ", unadjusted_fits_path)
}

# extract lmrob fits, dropping null model (fit_0)
lmrob_fits <- fits_raw %>%
  purrr::map(~ .x$robust_model_results$model_object) %>%
  tail(-1)

if (length(lmrob_fits) == 0) {
  stop("No models found after dropping the null model.")
}
if (is.null(names(lmrob_fits)) || any(names(lmrob_fits) == "")) {
  names(lmrob_fits) <- paste0("fit_", seq_along(lmrob_fits))
}

# extract OLS companion fits (pre-computed in step 7)
ols_fits <- fits_raw %>%
  purrr::map(~ .x$ols_model_results$model_object) %>%
  tail(-1)
names(ols_fits) <- names(lmrob_fits)

# record model count for downstream use
n_models <- length(lmrob_fits)
cat(glue("Loaded {n_models} substantive models (Models 1-{n_models}) from Step 7.\n\n"))


Loaded 11 substantive models (Models 1-11) from Step 7.
Code
# display verification summary
verify_msg <- glue(
  "<div class='callout-note'>
<strong>Step 7 Alignment Verification</strong><br><br>
<strong>Data source:</strong> <code>unadjusted_data.rds</code> ({nrow(unadjusted_data)} observations, {ncol(unadjusted_data)} variables)<br>
<strong>Model fits:</strong> <code>unadjusted_fits.rds</code> ({n_models} substantive models)<br>
<strong>Model names:</strong> {paste(names(lmrob_fits), collapse = ', ')}<br>
<strong>OLS companions:</strong> Pre-computed in Step 7 (not rebuilt)
</div>"
)
cat(verify_msg)







Note



Step 7 Alignment Verification Data source: unadjusted_data.rds (1045 observations, 17 variables) Model fits: unadjusted_fits.rds (11 substantive models) Model names: fit_1, fit_2, fit_3, fit_4, fit_5, fit_6, fit_7, fit_8, fit_9, fit_10, fit_11 OLS companions: Pre-computed in Step 7 (not rebuilt)

title: Influence Diagnostics & Sensitivity Sample
text:
1 Diagnostic Tests and Assumption Checks

1.1 Assessing Residual Shape


Code
# compute sample skewness and kurtosis without extra packages
skew_kurt <- function(x) {
  x <- x[is.finite(x)]
  n <- length(x)
  if (n < 4) {
    return(c(skewness = NA_real_, kurtosis = NA_real_))
  }

  m <- mean(x)
  s <- stats::sd(x)
  if (!is.finite(s) || s <= .Machine$double.eps) {
    return(c(skewness = NA_real_, kurtosis = NA_real_))
  }

  z <- (x - m) / s
  c(
    skewness = mean(z^3),
    kurtosis = mean(z^4)
  )
}




Code
# summarize standardized robust residuals for a single model
residual_shape <- function(fit) {
  r <- stats::residuals(fit) / fit$scale
  r <- r[is.finite(r)]

  if (length(r) == 0) {
    return(data.frame(
      n = 0L,
      mean = NA_real_,
      sd = NA_real_,
      median = NA_real_,
      MAD = NA_real_,
      skewness = NA_real_,
      kurtosis = NA_real_,
      p_gt2 = NA_real_,
      p_gt3 = NA_real_
    ))
  }

  sk <- skew_kurt(r)

  data.frame(
    n = length(r),
    mean = mean(r),
    sd = stats::sd(r),
    median = stats::median(r),
    MAD = stats::mad(r),
    skewness = unname(sk["skewness"]),
    kurtosis = unname(sk["kurtosis"]),
    p_gt2 = mean(abs(r) > 2),
    p_gt3 = mean(abs(r) > 3)
  )
}




Code
# table metadata for residual shape summary
col_names <- c(
  "Model", "$n$", "mean", "sd", "median", "MAD",

# create combined Q-Q plots using standardized robust residuals
combined_qq_plot <- function(models, labels, jitter = TRUE) {
  shapes <- (seq_along(models) - 1) %% 25

  plot_data <- purrr::map2_df(
    models, labels,
    ~ data.frame(std_resid = std_res(.x), Model = .y)
  )

  # preserve the supplied label order in the legend (default factor levels
  # sort alphabetically: 1, 10, 11, 2, ...)
  plot_data$Model <- factor(plot_data$Model, levels = unique(unlist(labels)))

  ggplot2::ggplot(plot_data, ggplot2::aes(
    sample = std_resid,
    colour = Model,
    shape = Model
  )) +
    ggplot2::stat_qq(
      size = 2,
      alpha = 0.8,
      position = if (jitter) ggplot2::position_jitter(width = 0.1) else ggplot2::position_identity()
    ) +
    ggplot2::geom_abline(
      intercept = 0,
      slope = 1,
      linetype = "dashed",
      linewidth = 0.5,
      color = "black"
    ) +
    viridis::scale_color_viridis(
      discrete = TRUE,
      option = "D",
      begin = 0.1,
      end = 0.9,
      name = "Model"
    ) +
    ggplot2::scale_shape_manual(values = shapes, name = "Model") +
    ggplot2::labs(
      x = "Theoretical Quantiles",
      y = "Standardized Robust Residuals"
    ) +
    ggplot2::guides(
      colour = ggplot2::guide_legend(nrow = 1, byrow = TRUE),
      shape = ggplot2::guide_legend(nrow = 1, byrow = TRUE)
    )
}

# create scale-location plot for a single robust model
scale_location_plot <- function(fit, name = NULL) {
  point_color <- viridis::viridis(4)[2]
  line_color <- viridis::viridis(4)[4]

  if (!"scale" %in% names(fit)) {
    stop("Model does not contain 'scale' component.")
  }

  std_resid <- stats::residuals(fit) / fit$scale

  model_data <- tibble::tibble(
    .fitted = stats::fitted(fit),
    sqrt_abs_std_resid = sqrt(abs(std_resid))
  )

  ggplot2::ggplot(model_data, ggplot2::aes(.fitted, sqrt_abs_std_resid)) +
    ggplot2::geom_point(alpha = 0.6, color = point_color) +
    ggplot2::geom_smooth(
      method = "loess",
      se = TRUE,
      formula = y ~ x,
      color = line_color,
      method.args = list(
        span = 1,
        degree = 1,
        family = "symmetric",
        control = stats::loess.control(surface = "direct")
      )
    ) +
    ggplot2::labs(
      title = name,
      x = "Fitted values",
      y = expression(sqrt("| Std. Residuals |"))
    )
}

# marginal model plot: outcome vs linear predictor (fitted values);

response_predictor_plot <- function(fit, name = NULL) {

1.5 Assessing Multicollinearity


Code
# compute VIF from model matrix
calc_vif <- function(mm) {
  int <- match("(Intercept)", colnames(mm), nomatch = 0L)
  if (int > 0L) {
    mm <- mm[, -int, drop = FALSE]
  }

  if (ncol(mm) < 2L) {
    return(setNames(numeric(0), character(0)))
  }

  ok <- rowSums(!is.finite(mm)) == 0L
  mm <- mm[ok, , drop = FALSE]

  if (nrow(mm) < 3L) {
    out <- rep(NA_real_, ncol(mm))
    names(out) <- colnames(mm)
    return(out)
  }

  vifs <- vapply(seq_len(ncol(mm)), function(j) {
    y <- mm[, j]
    x <- mm[, -j, drop = FALSE]

    sst <- sum((y - mean(y))^2)
    if (!is.finite(sst) || sst <= .Machine$double.eps) {
      return(NA_real_)
    }

    aux <- stats::lm.fit(x = cbind(1, x), y = y)
    rss <- sum(aux$residuals^2)
    r2 <- 1 - rss / sst
    r2 <- max(min(r2, 1), 0)

    if (r2 >= 1 - sqrt(.Machine$double.eps)) {
      return(Inf)
    }

    1 / (1 - r2)
  }, numeric(1))

  names(vifs) <- colnames(mm)
  vifs
}

# extract maximum VIF from a fitted model
get_max_vif <- function(fit) {
  mm <- tryCatch(stats::model.matrix(fit), error = function(e) NULL)
  if (is.null(mm)) {
    return(NA_real_)
  }

  vifs <- calc_vif(mm)
  if (length(vifs) == 0 || all(is.na(vifs))) {
    return(NA_real_)
  }

  max(vifs, na.rm = TRUE)
}

1.6 Identifying Influential Observations


Code
# extract participant IDs from fitted model
get_pid <- function(fit) {
  mf <- tryCatch(stats::model.frame(fit), error = function(e) NULL)
  pid <- if (!is.null(mf)) rownames(mf) else NULL

  if (is.null(pid) || anyNA(pid) || any(pid == "")) {
    pid <- as.character(seq_along(stats::residuals(fit)))
  }

  pid
}

# create long-format influence diagnostics for a single model
influence_long_df <- function(fit, model_name) {
  pid <- get_pid(fit)
  n <- length(pid)

  r <- stats::residuals(fit) / fit$scale
  h <- tryCatch(stats::hatvalues(fit), error = function(e) rep(NA_real_, n))
  w <- fit$rweights
  if (is.null(w)) {
    w <- rep(NA_real_, n)
  }

  p <- length(stats::coef(fit))
  lev_thr <- 2 * p / n

  score <- abs(r) * (h / pmax(1 - h, .Machine$double.eps))

  tibble::tibble(
    pid = as.character(pid),
    Model = model_name,
    std_resid = as.numeric(r),
    leverage = as.numeric(h),
    rweight = as.numeric(w),
    lev_thr = lev_thr,
    score = as.numeric(score)
  )
}

# map flagged pids back to data rows
match_ids_to_rows <- function(data, pids) {
  pids <- unique(as.character(pids))
  if (length(pids) == 0) {
    return(integer(0))
  }

  candidate_cols <- c("pid", "id", "obs_id", "row_id", "case_id", "participant_id", "idnummer")
  data_cols_lower <- tolower(names(data))

  for (nm in candidate_cols) {
    j <- match(nm, data_cols_lower, nomatch = 0L)
    if (j > 0L) {
      col <- names(data)[j]
      hits <- which(as.character(data[[col]]) %in% pids)
      if (length(hits) > 0) {
        return(hits)
      }
    }
  }

  if (!is.null(rownames(data))) {
    hits <- which(rownames(data) %in% pids)
    if (length(hits) > 0) {
      return(hits)
    }
  }

  idx <- suppressWarnings(as.integer(pids))
  if (!anyNA(idx) && all(idx >= 1) && all(idx <= nrow(data))) {
    return(idx)
  }

  integer(0)
}



in: lmrob_fits (per-model robust diagnostics)
out: influence_results$influential_obs, influence_results$influential_ids
rule: flag observation in a model if robust weight is below the global 5th percentile and \(|r|>2.5\), or if leverage exceeds \(2p/n\) and \(|r|>2.5\); mark as influential if flagged in at least 25% of models or if its maximum score reaches the 99th percentile



Code
# define flagging thresholds
alpha_weight <- 0.05
resid_cut <- 2.5

# compute influence diagnostics across all models
infl_long <- purrr::imap_dfr(lmrob_fits, ~ {
  influence_long_df(.x, model_name = gsub("fit_", "", .y))
})

# global robust-weight threshold (bottom 5% across all models)
w_thr <- stats::quantile(infl_long$rweight, probs = alpha_weight, na.rm = TRUE)

# apply flagging criteria
infl_long <- infl_long %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    low_weight = is.finite(rweight) & rweight < w_thr,
    high_leverage = is.finite(leverage) & leverage > lev_thr, # per-model 2p/n column
    large_resid = is.finite(std_resid) & abs(std_resid) > resid_cut,
    flag_model = (low_weight & large_resid) | (high_leverage & large_resid)
  )

k <- length(lmrob_fits)

# aggregate influence flags across models
infl_summary <- infl_long %>%
  dplyr::group_by(pid) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    n_flag = sum(flag_model, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_abs_resid = max(abs(std_resid), na.rm = TRUE),
    max_leverage = max(leverage, na.rm = TRUE),
    min_rweight = min(rweight, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_score = max(score, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# identify influential observations using combined criteria
score_thr <- stats::quantile(infl_summary$max_score, probs = 0.99, na.rm = TRUE)
flag_min_models <- ceiling(k * 0.25)

influential_obs <- infl_summary %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    influential = (n_flag >= flag_min_models) | (max_score >= score_thr)
  ) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(influential), dplyr::desc(n_flag), dplyr::desc(max_score))

influential_ids <- influential_obs %>%
  dplyr::filter(influential) %>%
  dplyr::pull(pid) %>%
  unique()

# store results
influence_results <- list(
  influential_obs = influential_obs,
  influential_ids = influential_ids,
  thresholds = list(
    w_thr = w_thr,
    resid_cut = resid_cut,
    lev_rule = "2p/n",
    score_thr = score_thr,
    min_models = flag_min_models
  )
)

htmltools::HTML(glue(

  "Residual cut: |r| > {resid_cut}. ",
  "Leverage rule: {influence_results$thresholds$lev_rule}. ",
  "Across-model flag: at least {influence_results$thresholds$min_models} of {k} models or max score above the 99th percentile.",
  "</p>"
))

Note:   Rows are aggregated across models. ‘n_flag’ is the number of models where the observation meets the within-model rule (low weight and large residual, or high leverage and large residual). ‘max score’ is max(|r| × h/(1-h)) across models and is used as an additional screen.















Code
# create influence diagnostic panel for a single model
plot_influence_panel <- function(df, flagged_ids, name = NULL) {
  df <- df %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      flagged = pid %in% flagged_ids,
      label = ifelse(flagged, pid, NA_character_),
      influence = 2 / pmax(1 - leverage, .Machine$double.eps)
    )

  breaks <- unique(stats::quantile(df$influence, c(0, 0.33, 0.67, 1), na.rm = TRUE))
  breaks <- breaks[is.finite(breaks)]
  n_intervals <- length(breaks) - 1

  if (n_intervals <= 0) {
    df$influence_cat <- factor("Low", levels = "Low")
  } else {
    labels <- c("Low", "Medium", "High")[seq_len(n_intervals)]
    df$influence_cat <- cut(
      df$influence,
      breaks = breaks,
      labels = labels,
      include.lowest = TRUE
    )
  }

  p <- ggplot2::ggplot(df, ggplot2::aes(leverage, std_resid, color = influence_cat)) +
    ggplot2::geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
    ggplot2::geom_smooth(
      method = "loess",
      se = TRUE,
      alpha = 0.3,
      formula = y ~ x,
      method.args = list(
        span = 1,
        degree = 1,
        family = "symmetric",
        control = stats::loess.control(surface = "direct")
      )
    ) +
    ggplot2::labs(
      title = name,
      x = "Leverage",
      y = "Standardized Residuals",
      color = "Influence"
    ) +
    ggplot2::scale_color_viridis_d(option = "D", direction = -1) +
    ggplot2::guides(color = ggplot2::guide_legend(title = "Influence"))

  if (requireNamespace("ggrepel", quietly = TRUE) && any(df$flagged, na.rm = TRUE)) {
    p <- p + ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
      ggplot2::aes(label = label),
      size = 2,
      max.overlaps = 20,
      na.rm = TRUE
    )
  }

  p
}



Code
flagged_ids <- influence_results$influential_ids

# split() orders panels alphabetically (1, 10, 11, 2, ...); restore numeric order
infl_split <- split(infl_long, infl_long$Model)
infl_split <- infl_split[order(as.integer(names(infl_split)))]
invisible(purrr::iwalk(infl_split, ~ print(
  plot_influence_panel(.x, flagged_ids, name = paste0("Model ", .y))
)))

Code
# clear global env and force full garbage collection
rm(list = ls(envir = .GlobalEnv), envir = .GlobalEnv)
invisible(gc(full = TRUE))

# detach non-base packages to avoid namespace interference
invisible(lapply(grep(":|autoloads|globalenv", search(),
  invert = TRUE, value = TRUE, ignore.case = TRUE
), detach, character.only = TRUE, unload = TRUE, force = TRUE))

# source custom functions for data handling and analysis
purrr::walk(file.path(here::here("assets", "functions"), paste0(c(
  "summarize_na", "set_globals", "describe", "import_pipes",
  "add_style", "beautify_names", "as_kable", "format_p_value",
  "footnote", "md_to_html", "numeric_df"
), ".R")), source)

# set global options and import pipe operators
set_globals()
import_pipes()

# load imputed dataset from step 5
imputed_data <- haven::read_sav(
  here::here("assets", "data", "saved", "imputed_data_combined.sav")
)

3 SES Variable Structure


Code
col_names <- c("Variable", "Present", "$n_{valid}$", "$\\%_{valid}$")
footnote_text <- glue("$N = {n_obs}$")

ses_structure <- data.frame(
  Variable = c(
    "education_self", "education_partner", "education_father",
    "education_mother", "education_parental",
    "isei08_self", "isei08_partner", "isei08_father",
    "isei08_mother", "isei08_parental"
  ),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Present = ifelse(Variable %in% names(imputed_data),
      "$\\checkmark$", "$\\times$"
    ),
    n_valid = sapply(Variable, function(v) {
      if (v %in% names(imputed_data)) {
        sum(!is.na(imputed_data[[v]]))
      } else {
        NA_integer_
      }
    }),
    pct_valid = sapply(Variable, function(v) {
      if (v %in% names(imputed_data)) {
        round(100 * mean(!is.na(imputed_data[[v]])), 1)
      } else {
        NA_real_
      }
    })
  )

ses_structure$Present %<>% md_to_html()

ses_structure %>%
  as_kable() %>%
  footnote() %>%
  add_style()

title: Post-Imputation Data Finalization
text:
4 Summarize Missing Data Post-Imputation
After imputation, only structural missingness should remain (partner variables for non-partnered respondents); the passthrough egp11 columns also retain missingness by design, since class codes track the occupation codes and are never imputed.


Code
col_names <- c("Variable", "$n_{miss}$", "$\\%_{miss}$", "Structural$^{1}$")

footnote_text <- glue(
  "$N = {n_obs}$; ",
  "$^{{1}}$ Structural missingness = partner-specific variables for non-partnered respondents."
)




Code
# summarize missingness for key analysis variables
missing_summary <- imputed_data %>%
  dplyr::select(dplyr::any_of(all_expected)) %>%
  summarize_na() %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    variable = beautify_names(variable),
    structural = ifelse(
      grepl("partner", variable, ignore.case = TRUE) & n_miss > 0,
      "Yes", "No"
    )
  )

missing_summary %>%
  as_kable() %>%
  footnote() %>%
  add_style()

6.2 Non-Partnered Respondents
Partner SES stays NA for non-partnered respondents in this dataset. Step 7 conditions these variables on partnership (zero for non-partnered) and enters them as interactions with the partner indicator, so every model retains the full sample while partner SES effects are identified by partnered respondents only.


Code
# prepare non-partnered data, removing partner-specific variables
non_partnered_data <- imputed_data %>%
  dplyr::filter(partner == 0) %>%
  dplyr::select(
    -idnummer, -partner,
    -dplyr::any_of(c("education_partner", "isei08_partner"))
  )

# remove columns with all NA values (if any remain)
non_partnered_data <- non_partnered_data %>%
  dplyr::select(where(~ !all(is.na(.x))))

non_partnered_data %>%
  setNames(beautify_names(names(.))) %>%
  describe(
    normality = TRUE,
    print_only = FALSE
  ) %>%
  dplyr::rowwise() %>%
  dplyr::ungroup() %>%
  dplyr::select(-p) %>%
  as_kable() %>%
  footnote() %>%
  add_style()

title: Post-Imputation Data Finalization
text:
8 Variable Summary


Code
col_names <- c(
  "Variable", "Type", "$n_{valid}$", "$n_{miss}$",
  "$\\%_{miss}$", "$n_{unique}$", "Category"
)

footnote_text <- glue("$N = {n_obs}$")




Code
# create comprehensive variable summary
var_summary <- data.frame(
  variable = names(imputed_data),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    type = sapply(imputed_data, function(x) {
      if (is.numeric(x)) "Numeric" else if (is.factor(x)) "Factor" else class(x)[1]
    }),
    n_valid = sapply(imputed_data, function(x) sum(!is.na(x))),
    n_miss = sapply(imputed_data, function(x) sum(is.na(x))),
    pct_miss = round(100 * n_miss / n_obs, 1),
    n_unique = sapply(imputed_data, function(x) length(unique(x[!is.na(x)]))),
    category = dplyr::case_when(
      variable == "idnummer" ~ "Identifier",
      grepl("^education", variable) ~ "Education",
      grepl("^isei08", variable) ~ "Occupation (ISEI-08)",
      grepl("^isco08r", variable) ~ "Occupation (ISCO-08)",
      grepl("^brc14", variable) ~ "Occupation (BRC-14)",
      grepl("^dhd", variable) ~ "Dietary Outcome",
      grepl("income", variable) ~ "Income",
      grepl("partner", variable) ~ "Partner Status",
      TRUE ~ "Other"
    )
  ) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(category, variable) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(variable = beautify_names(variable))

var_summary %>%
  as_kable() %>%
  footnote() %>%
  add_style()

title: Detecting & Removing Outliers
text:
Code
# clear global env and force full garbage collection
rm(list = ls(envir = .GlobalEnv), envir = .GlobalEnv)
invisible(gc(full = TRUE))

# detach non-base packages to avoid namespace interference
invisible(lapply(grep(":|autoloads|globalenv", search(),
  invert = TRUE, value = TRUE, ignore.case = TRUE
), detach, character.only = TRUE, unload = TRUE, force = TRUE))

# source custom functions for data handling and visualization
purrr::walk(file.path(here::here("assets", "functions"), paste0(c(
  "remove_invariant", "md_to_html", "set_globals", "add_style",
  "format_numeric", "as_kable", "format_p_value", "import_pipes",
  "footnote", "beautify_names", "numeric_df", "describe"
), ".R")), source)

# set global options and import pipe operators
set_globals()
import_pipes()

# load dataset prepared for imputation (from step 3)
main <- haven::read_sav(
  here::here("assets", "data", "saved", "data_for_imputation.sav"),
  user_na = FALSE
)
Code
# partner-specific variables with structural missingness (NA by design for non-partnered)
partner_vars <- c("education_partner", "isei08_partner")

# parental SES composites from step 3 (dimension_referent convention)
ses_composites <- c("education_parental", "isei08_parental")

# target variables for univariate outlier detection
univariate_targets <- list(
  dhd_kcal = "iqr",
  dhd_index = "mad"
)

title: Detecting & Removing Outliers
Detecting & Removing Outliers
text:
2 Clean Dataset for Outlier Detection


Code
# remove invariant columns and replace non-finite values with NA
clean_data <- main %>%
  # reconciled score, class, and status columns stay out of outlier
  # detection entirely: siops08 and egp11 would shrink the complete-case
  # pool through their missingness and duplicate the occupational signal
  # already carried by isei08; lf_status is character
  dplyr::select(-dplyr::matches("^siops08|^egp11|^lf_status")) %>%
  remove_invariant() %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    dplyr::across(
      dplyr::where(is.numeric),
      ~ ifelse(is.finite(.x), .x, NA)
    )
  ) %>%
  # select numeric columns with non-zero variance
  dplyr::select(dplyr::where(~ is.numeric(.x) && sd(.x, na.rm = TRUE) != 0))

# identify partner-specific variables present in cleaned data
partner_vars_in_data <- intersect(partner_vars, names(clean_data))

# retain complete cases EXCLUDING partner-specific variables
# this preserves non-partnered respondents with legitimate NA values
clean_data <- if (length(partner_vars_in_data) > 0) {
  clean_data %>%
    dplyr::filter(complete.cases(
      dplyr::select(., -dplyr::any_of(partner_vars_in_data))
    ))
} else {
  clean_data %>%
    dplyr::filter(complete.cases(.))
}

# record sample sizes
n_original <- nrow(main)
n_clean <- nrow(clean_data)
n_excluded_cleaning <- n_original - n_clean




Code
cat(glue(
  "<p><strong>Data Cleaning Summary:</strong> ",
  "Of {n_original} observations in the input dataset, ",
  "{n_clean} ({round(100 * n_clean / n_original, 1)}%) were retained for ",
  "outlier detection after removing cases with missing values on substantive ",
  "variables. Partner-specific variables were excluded from this filter to ",
  "preserve non-partnered respondents.</p>"
))


Data Cleaning Summary: Of 1059 observations in the input dataset, 351 (33.1%) were retained for outlier detection after removing cases with missing values on substantive variables. Partner-specific variables were excluded from this filter to preserve non-partnered respondents.

title: Detecting & Removing Outliers
Detecting & Removing Outliers
text:
4 Detect Multivariate Outliers


Code
# detect multivariate outliers using Local Outlier Factor (LOF)
# k is fixed at 20 to avoid the default combined-k behavior of bigutilsr::LOF,
# which aggregates across seq_k = c(4, 10, 30) and complicates interpretation
multivariate_outliers <- function(df, excl = NULL,
                                  id_var = "idnummer", k = 20L) {
  stopifnot(id_var %in% names(df))

  # add row numbers for tracking
  df_with_id <- df %>%
    dplyr::mutate(orig_row = dplyr::row_number())

  # prepare data: remove invariant columns, replace non-finite with NA
  df_clean <- df_with_id %>%
    remove_invariant() %>%
    dplyr::mutate(dplyr::across(
      dplyr::where(is.numeric),
      ~ ifelse(is.finite(.x), .x, NA)
    )) %>%
    dplyr::select(dplyr::where(~ is.numeric(.x) && sd(.x, na.rm = TRUE) != 0)) %>%
    dplyr::filter(complete.cases(.))

  df_clean <- numeric_df(df_clean)

  # keep id mapping separate from LOF feature set
  id_map <- df_clean %>%
    dplyr::select(dplyr::all_of(c(id_var, "orig_row")))

  # exclude identifiers and specified variables from LOF features
  excl_all <- unique(c(excl, id_var, "orig_row"))
  lof_df <- df_clean %>%
    dplyr::select(-dplyr::any_of(excl_all))

  if (ncol(lof_df) == 0) {
    stop("No numeric variables left for LOF after exclusions.")
  }

  # compute log-LOF with single, explicit k (Breunig et al., 2000).
  # with seq_k of length 1, `combine` is irrelevant because only one score
  # exists per observation; we keep the default `combine = max` for
  # compatibility with bigutilsr's API.
  llof <- bigutilsr::LOF(
    U = as.matrix(lof_df),
    seq_k = k,
    log = TRUE
  )

  # Tukey's adjusted boxplot rule (skew-corrected medcouple correction) is
  # the idiomatic bigutilsr cutoff for LOF output (see `?tukey_mc_up`).
  # Default alpha = 0.05 controls Type-I error after multiple-testing
  # correction; this is the canonical usage in the bigutilsr manual and is
  # retained here rather than tightening to the legacy thresh = 0.001,
  # which corresponds to a percentile rule, not a Type-I error.
  lof_cutoff <- bigutilsr::tukey_mc_up(llof)
  lof_outs_idxs <- which(llof > lof_cutoff)

  # map back to original ids
  out_rows <- id_map$orig_row[lof_outs_idxs]
  out_ids <- id_map[[id_var]][lof_outs_idxs]

  # filter out outliers by id
  df_without_outs <- df %>%
    dplyr::filter(!.data[[id_var]] %in% out_ids)

  list(
    df = df_without_outs,
    llof = llof,
    cutoff = lof_cutoff,
    k = k,
    id = out_ids,
    row = out_rows,
    n_features = ncol(lof_df),
    features_used = names(lof_df)
  )
}

title: Detecting & Removing Outliers
text:
5 Remove Detected Outliers

5.1 Univariate Outlier Removal


Code
# detect univariate outliers
univariate_analysis <- clean_data %>%
  detect_outliers(
    vars = names(univariate_targets),
    meths = unlist(univariate_targets)
  )

# extract components
uni_summary <- univariate_analysis$summary
uni_results <- univariate_analysis$results
uni_dropped <- univariate_analysis$updated_data %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(outlier))
uni_kept <- univariate_analysis$updated_data %>%
  dplyr::filter(is.na(outlier))




5.2 Multivariate Outlier Removal


Code
# identify columns to exclude from LOF based on variance/uniqueness
cols_low_variance <- names(uni_kept)[sapply(uni_kept, function(x) {
  is.numeric(x) && (length(unique(x)) <= 2 ||
    length(unique(round(x))) > 0.95 * nrow(uni_kept))
})]

# partner-specific variables to exclude (structural missingness)
# uses canonical dimension_referent naming convention
partner_vars_exclude <- c("education_partner", "isei08_partner", "partner")

# combine all exclusions
cols_to_exclude <- unique(c(
  cols_low_variance,
  partner_vars_exclude[partner_vars_exclude %in% names(uni_kept)]
))

# document excluded columns
if (length(cols_to_exclude) > 0) {
  excluded_low_var <- cols_low_variance[cols_low_variance %in% names(uni_kept)]
  excluded_partner <- partner_vars_exclude[partner_vars_exclude %in% names(uni_kept)]

  exclusion_parts <- c()
  if (length(excluded_low_var) > 0) {
    exclusion_parts <- c(exclusion_parts, glue(
      "low variance/near-constant/near-unique: {toString(dQuote(excluded_low_var, q = FALSE))}"
    ))
  }
  if (length(excluded_partner) > 0) {
    exclusion_parts <- c(exclusion_parts, glue(
      "structural missingness (partner-specific): {toString(dQuote(excluded_partner, q = FALSE))}"
    ))
  }

  excluded_msg <- glue(
    "<p><strong>Note.</strong> Columns excluded from multivariate outlier detection:<br>",
    "{paste(paste0('• ', exclusion_parts), collapse = '<br>')}</p>"
  )
} else {
  excluded_msg <- "<p><strong>Note.</strong> No columns were excluded from multivariate outlier detection.</p>"
}
cat(excluded_msg)


Note. Columns excluded from multivariate outlier detection: • low variance/near-constant/near-unique: “idnummer”, “normal_week”, “home_owner”, “woman”, “partner”• structural missingness (partner-specific): “education_partner”, “isei08_partner”, “partner”


Code
# detect and remove multivariate outliers
outliers_removed <- multivariate_outliers(
  df = uni_kept,
  excl = cols_to_exclude
)

lof_scores <- outliers_removed$llof
lof_cutoff <- outliers_removed$cutoff
ids_removed <- outliers_removed$id
n_lof_features <- outliers_removed$n_features

# collect multivariate outliers from original dataset
multi_dropped <- main %>%
  dplyr::filter(idnummer %in% ids_removed) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(outlier = "Multivariate")

# combine univariate and multivariate outliers
cases_with_outliers <- dplyr::bind_rows(uni_dropped, multi_dropped) %>%
  dplyr::distinct(idnummer, .keep_all = TRUE)

# calculate removal counts
rows_to_remove_uni <- nrow(uni_dropped)
rows_to_remove_multi <- nrow(multi_dropped)
expected_removed <- rows_to_remove_uni + rows_to_remove_multi
actual_removed <- nrow(cases_with_outliers)

# check for overlap
overlap_count <- expected_removed - actual_removed
if (overlap_count > 0) {
  cat(glue(
    "<p><strong>Note.</strong> {overlap_count} case(s) were flagged by both ",
    "univariate and multivariate methods and are counted once in the total.</p>"
  ))
}

cat(glue(
  "<p><strong>LOF detection (k = {outliers_removed$k}):</strong> ",
  "{length(ids_removed)} multivariate outlier(s) identified via Tukey's ",
  "adjusted boxplot rule on log-LOF scores (bigutilsr::tukey_mc_up, ",
  "default alpha = 0.05 after multiple-testing correction; ",
  "log-LOF cutoff = {round(lof_cutoff, 3)}; max observed log-LOF = ",
  "{round(max(lof_scores, na.rm = TRUE), 3)}).</p>"
))


LOF detection (k = 20): 0 multivariate outlier(s) identified via Tukey’s adjusted boxplot rule on log-LOF scores (bigutilsr::tukey_mc_up, default alpha = 0.05 after multiple-testing correction; log-LOF cutoff = 0.747; max observed log-LOF = 0.632).




Code
cat(glue(
  "<p><strong>LOF Detection Summary:</strong> Multivariate outlier detection ",
  "was performed using {n_lof_features} numeric features after exclusions.</p>"
))


LOF Detection Summary: Multivariate outlier detection was performed using 26 numeric features after exclusions.

title: Detecting & Removing Outliers
text:
6 Summarize Outlier Detection Results

6.1 Univariate Outlier Summary


Code
# table metadata for univariate outlier summary
univariate_tbl_meta <- list(
  col_names = c(
    "$\\ $", "$n$", "$\\text{Method}$", "$\\bar{x}$",
    "$CI_{\\text{lower}}$", "$CI_{\\text{upper}}$",
    "$T^{+}$", "$T^{-}$"
  ),
  footnote_text = glue(
    "Outliers were detected using the Interquartile Range (IQR) ",
    "method for DHD kcal, which identifies extreme deviations based ",
    "on quartiles, and the Median Absolute Deviation (MAD) method ",
    "for DHD index, emphasizing robust measures of spread. ",
    "Threshold values $T^+$ (upper) and $T^-$ (lower) are ",
    "calculated from these methods to classify outliers. ",
    "'Total detections' sums all flagged values; 'Unique cases' ",
    "counts distinct respondents (some may be flagged on multiple variables)."
  )
)

# assign to parent frame for use by as_kable and footnote
col_names <- univariate_tbl_meta$col_names
footnote_text <- univariate_tbl_meta$footnote_text




Code
data.frame(
  var = uni_summary$variable,
  count = uni_summary$count
) %>%
  cbind(uni_summary$tbl) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(var = beautify_names(var)) %>%
  format_numeric() %>%
  as_kable() %>%
  footnote() %>%
  add_style()

6.3 Combined Outlier Cases


Code
footnote_text <- glue(
  "$N_{{\\text{{total}}}} = {actual_removed}$; ",
  "$N_{{\\text{{univariate}}}} = {rows_to_remove_uni}$; ",
  "$N_{{\\text{{multivariate}}}} = {rows_to_remove_multi}$.<br>",
  "Cases were excluded using two independent outlier detection approaches:<br>",
  "1) Univariate outliers: Identified via boxplot analysis with variable-specific ",
  "thresholds (DHD-kcal: $Q_1 - 1.5 \\times IQR$ and $Q_3 + 1.5 \\times IQR$; ",
  "DHD-index: median $\\pm 2.5 \\times MAD$).<br>",
  "2) Multivariate outliers: Detected using Local Outlier Factor (LOF) method ",
  "(Tukey's adjusted boxplot cutoff on log-LOF scores) on {n_lof_features} numeric features ",
  "with non-zero variance and complete cases. ",
  "Partner-specific variables were excluded due to structural missingness. ",
  "Reconciled score and status columns were excluded from detection to keep ",
  "the pool and feature set comparable with the pre-reconciliation analysis."
)

    outlier = gsub("MultiVariableiate", "Multivariate", outlier, fixed = TRUE)

  footnote() %>%

title: ISCO-08 Standardization and Validation
text:
Code
# clear global env and force full garbage collection
rm(list = ls(envir = .GlobalEnv), envir = .GlobalEnv)
invisible(gc(full = TRUE))

# detach non-base packages to avoid namespace interference
invisible(lapply(grep(":|autoloads|globalenv", search(),
  invert = TRUE, value = TRUE, ignore.case = TRUE
), detach, character.only = TRUE, unload = TRUE, force = TRUE))

# source custom functions for text processing and data handling
purrr::walk(file.path(here::here("assets", "functions"), paste0(c(
  "sci_to_tex", "md_to_html", "set_globals", "decapitalize",
  "save_function", "add_style", "suppress_output", "import_pipes",
  "numeric_df", "as_kable", "empty_to_na", "drop_all_na_rows",
  "named_list", "std_text", "beautify_names", "footnote"
), ".R")), source)

# set global options and import pipe operators
set_globals()
import_pipes()

title: ISCO-08 Standardization and Validation
text:
1 Define Constants
Respondent-type suffixes govern column naming throughout the step.


Code
# suffixes map respondent roles to column name patterns
# empty string for respondent self, underscore-prefixed for others
suffixes <- c("partner", "mother", "father") %>%
  setNames(object = sprintf("_%s", .)) %>%
  c("respondent" = "", .)

# occupation column names derived from suffixes
occupation_columns <- paste0("occupation", unname(suffixes))

title: ISCO-08 Standardization and Validation
text:
2 Load Data


Code
# load initial dataset and rename ISCO-08 columns for clarity
main <- haven::read_sav(here::here("assets", "data", "main_initial.sav")) %>%
  dplyr::rename(
    isco08_old = BRC14,
    isco08_old_father = BRC14_father,
    isco08_old_mother = BRC14_mother,
    isco08_old_partner = BRC14_partner
  )

# codes are four-character zero-padded text at every joining layer
pad4 <- function(x) {
  x <- trimws(as.character(x))
  ifelse(is.na(x) | x %in% c("", "NA"), NA_character_,
         formatC(as.integer(x), width = 4, flag = "0"))
}

# --- audited coding with attached scores, labels, and review record ---
scored <- readxl::read_excel(
  here::here("assets", "data", "isco08_codes_scored.xlsx"), col_types = "text"
) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(isco08 = pad4(isco08),
                dplyr::across(c(isei08, siops08), as.numeric))

# --- curation workbook: supplies english group labels for delivered codes ---
curated <- readxl::read_excel(
  here::here("assets", "data", "isco08_codes.xlsx"), col_types = "text"
) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(isco08_init = pad4(isco08_init),
                isco08_new = pad4(isco08_new))

# english unit-group labels, coalesced across both workbooks
label_map <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  scored %>% dplyr::select(code = isco08, label = isco08_label_en),
  curated %>% dplyr::select(code = isco08_init, label = isco08_init_group),
  curated %>% dplyr::select(code = isco08_new, label = isco08_new_group)
) %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(code), !is.na(label)) %>%
  dplyr::distinct(code, .keep_all = TRUE)

isco_label <- function(code) {
  out <- label_map$label[match(code, label_map$code)]
  ifelse(is.na(out), "", out)
}

brc <- readr::read_csv(
  here::here("assets", "data", "brc2014_isco08_brc_crosswalk.csv"),
  col_types = readr::cols(.default = "c")
) %>%
  dplyr::transmute(isco08 = pad4(ISCO2008unitgroup),
                   brc14  = as.numeric(BRC2014beroepsgroep))

egp <- readr::read_csv(
  here::here("assets", "data", "egp_lookup.csv"), show_col_types = FALSE
) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(isco08 = pad4(isco08)) %>%
  dplyr::select(isco08, egp11)

# every code must exist in brc2014; never invent codes
stopifnot(all(scored$isco08[!is.na(scored$isco08)] %in% brc$isco08))

title: ISCO-08 Standardization and Validation
text:
3 Clean and Standardize Data

in: main
out: main_with_copies with _init and _cleaned occupation columns
rule: set two invalid entries (idnummer 2023, 1854) to NA; standardize text via std_text()



Code
# replace invalid occupation entries with NA (case-specific corrections)
main[main$idnummer == 2023, "occupation_mother"] <- NA
main[main$idnummer == 1854, "occupation_partner"] <- NA

# generate initial and cleaned copies of occupation data
main_with_copies <- main %>%
  dplyr::mutate(dplyr::across(
    tidyselect::all_of(occupation_columns),
    list(
      init = ~.,
      cleaned = ~ std_text(.)
    ),
    .names = "{col}_{fn}"
  ))

title: ISCO-08 Standardization and Validation
text:
4 Integrate Audited ISCO Codes

in: scored, brc, egp, main_with_copies
out: main with joined isco08r*, brc14*, isei08*, siops08*, egp11*, lf_status* columns
rule: typed one-to-one join on idnummer from the audited (idnummer, source) coding; the respondent columns carry no suffix; the render stops on row inflation, duplicated idnummer, or any coded report without a score



Code
main <- main_with_copies

# --- one row per person, pipeline-conformant names (respondent carries no suffix) ---
suffix_map <- c(respondent = "", father = "_father",
                mother = "_mother", partner = "_partner")
scores_wide <- scored %>%
  dplyr::left_join(brc, by = "isco08") %>%
  dplyr::left_join(egp, by = "isco08") %>%
  dplyr::transmute(
    idnummer  = as.integer(idnummer),
    suffix    = unname(suffix_map[source]),
    isco08r   = as.numeric(isco08),
    brc14, isei08, siops08, egp11,
    lf_status
  ) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(
    id_cols     = idnummer,
    names_from  = suffix,
    values_from = c(isco08r, brc14, isei08, siops08, egp11, lf_status),
    names_glue  = "{.value}{suffix}"
  )

# --- typed one-to-one join keyed on the integer identifier ---
n_before <- nrow(main)
main <- main %>%
  dplyr::mutate(idnummer = as.integer(idnummer)) %>%
  dplyr::left_join(scores_wide, by = "idnummer")

stopifnot(
  nrow(main) == n_before,
  !any(duplicated(main$idnummer)),
  # full score coverage on every coded report, armed forces included
  !any(is.na(main$isei08[!is.na(main$isco08r)])),
  !any(is.na(main$isei08_father[!is.na(main$isco08r_father)])),
  !any(is.na(main$isei08_mother[!is.na(main$isco08r_mother)])),
  !any(is.na(main$isei08_partner[!is.na(main$isco08r_partner)]))
)

# persons with delivered codes but no row in the coding file blank via the join;
# their reports carry no codable occupation text
n_absent <- sum(!main$idnummer %in% as.integer(scored$idnummer))

message(sprintf(
  "joined audited coding: %d persons; coded self %d, father %d, mother %d, partner %d; %d persons absent from the coding file.",
  nrow(main),
  sum(!is.na(main$isco08r)), sum(!is.na(main$isco08r_father)),
  sum(!is.na(main$isco08r_mother)), sum(!is.na(main$isco08r_partner)),
  n_absent
))

title: ISCO-08 Standardization and Validation
text:
5 Validate Attached Scores

rule: the joined per-source coded counts must equal the coding file exactly; where the ISCO08ConveRsions package can convert a code, its ISEI-08 and SIOPS-08 values are compared against the attached scores as an independent route (differences are reported, never silently accepted)



Code
# per-source coded counts in the joined data must match the coding file
counts_file <- scored %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(isco08)) %>%
  dplyr::count(source) %>%
  tibble::deframe()
counts_joined <- c(
  respondent = sum(!is.na(main$isco08r)),
  father     = sum(!is.na(main$isco08r_father)),
  mother     = sum(!is.na(main$isco08r_mother)),
  partner    = sum(!is.na(main$isco08r_partner))
)
stopifnot(identical(counts_joined[names(counts_file)], counts_file))

# independent conversion route, non-halting: the package rejects code 0000
# (armed forces, not further specified), which the attached scores cover
if (requireNamespace("ISCO08ConveRsions", quietly = TRUE)) {
  safe_num <- function(f) function(z) tryCatch(f(z), error = function(e) NA_real_)
  concordance <- scored %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(isco08)) %>%
    dplyr::distinct(isco08, isei08, siops08) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      pkg_isei  = vapply(isco08, safe_num(ISCO08ConveRsions::isco08toisei08), numeric(1)),
      pkg_siops = vapply(isco08, safe_num(ISCO08ConveRsions::isco08tosiops08), numeric(1))
    )
  n_checked <- sum(!is.na(concordance$pkg_isei))
  n_diff <- sum(
    (!is.na(concordance$pkg_isei) & abs(concordance$pkg_isei - concordance$isei08) > 0.01) |
    (!is.na(concordance$pkg_siops) & abs(concordance$pkg_siops - concordance$siops08) > 0.01)
  )
  message(sprintf(
    "score concordance: %d of %d distinct codes convertible by ISCO08ConveRsions; %d disagreement(s) with the attached scores.",
    n_checked, nrow(concordance), n_diff
  ))
  if (n_diff > 0) print(dplyr::filter(
    concordance,
    (!is.na(pkg_isei) & abs(pkg_isei - isei08) > 0.01) |
    (!is.na(pkg_siops) & abs(pkg_siops - siops08) > 0.01)
  ))
} else {
  message("ISCO08ConveRsions not installed; skipping the independent score cross-check.")
}


score concordance: 342 of 342 distinct codes convertible by ISCO08ConveRsions; 0 disagreement(s) with the attached scores.

title: ISCO-08 Standardization and Validation
text:
6 Finalize Analytical Dataset

in: main (with the joined audited coding and scores)
out: main_corrected.sav
rule: empty strings to NA; occupation text set to NA where the final ISCO-08 code is NA



Code
# replace empty strings with NA in character columns
remove_empty_strings <- function(data_frame) {
  dplyr::mutate(
    data_frame,
    dplyr::across(
      dplyr::where(is.character),
      ~ dplyr::if_else(.x == "", NA_character_, .x)
    )
  )
}

# clean empty strings and standardize column names on the joined dataset
main_cleaned <- main %>%
  remove_empty_strings() %>%
  dplyr::select(-dplyr::any_of(c("source", "imputed"))) %>%
  setNames(decapitalize(names(.)))

# guard against silent row loss or inflation relative to the pre-join frame
stopifnot(nrow(main_cleaned) == nrow(main_with_copies))

# ensure occupation aligns with final ISCO codes
for (suffix in unname(suffixes)) {
  isco_col <- paste0("isco08r", suffix)
  occ_col <- paste0("occupation", suffix)
  main_cleaned[[occ_col]][is.na(main_cleaned[[isco_col]])] <- NA
}
main <- main_cleaned

# export corrected dataset for subsequent use
haven::write_sav(main, here::here("assets", "data", "saved", "main_corrected.sav"))

title: ISCO-08 Standardization and Validation
text:
7 Visualize Classification Corrections

Code
# scatter plot with regression line to visualize code corrections
scatter <- function(x_var, y_var, title, point_size = 2) {
  # filter complete cases for plotting
  df_clean <- main %>%
    dplyr::select(dplyr::all_of(c(x_var, y_var))) %>%
    dplyr::filter(complete.cases(.))

  # compute linear model for trend visualization
  fit <- lm(as.formula(paste(y_var, "~", x_var)), data = df_clean)

  # add residuals and predictions to dataset
  df_clean$resids <- residuals(fit)
  df_clean$y_hat <- predict(fit)
  df_clean$resids_trans <- sqrt(abs(df_clean$resids))

  # generate scatter plot with color-coded deviations
  df_clean %>%
    ggplot2::ggplot(ggplot2::aes(
      x = .data[[x_var]],
      y = .data[[y_var]],
      color = resids_trans
    )) +
    ggplot2::geom_smooth(
      method = "lm",
      se = FALSE,
      color = "black",
      linewidth = 0.8,
      formula = y ~ x
    ) +
    ggplot2::geom_jitter(
      size = point_size,
      alpha = 0.6,
      width = 0.2,
      height = 0.2
    ) +
    ggplot2::scale_color_viridis_c(option = "plasma", name = "Deviation") +
    ggplot2::labs(
      title = title,
      x = "ISCO-08 (Corrected)",
      y = "ISCO-08 (Original)"
    )
}

# plot comparisons for each respondent type
scatter("isco08r", "isco08_old", "Respondent")
scatter("isco08r_partner", "isco08_old_partner", "Partner")
scatter("isco08r_father", "isco08_old_father", "Father")
scatter("isco08r_mother", "isco08_old_mother", "Mother")




















Comparison of the Initial and Corrected ISCO-08 Codes

title: ISCO-08 Standardization and Validation
text:
8 Summarize ISCO Corrections
Changed digits appear in red and unchanged digits in purple within each correction table. A blank corrected code means the report was reviewed and set to not codable; the justification records why. The summary table is computed over every person in the dataset; persons without any row in the coding file (no codable occupation text in any report) have their delivered codes blanked by the join and do not appear in the correction tables.


Code
# apply HTML formatting to column names (reduce bracket font size)
format_brackets <- function(vec) {
  stringr::str_replace_all(vec, c(
    "\\(([^)]+)\\)" = "<span style='font-size: 0.8em; white-space: nowrap;'>(\\1)</span>",
    "ISCO-08" = "ISCO‑08"
  ))
}

# column headers for ISCO corrections table
col_names <- format_brackets(c(
  "ID No.",
  "Occupation (as reported)$\\ ^1$",
  "ISCO-08 (original)",
  "ISCO-08 Group (original)",
  "ISCO-08 (corrected)",
  "ISCO-08 Group (corrected)",
  "Justification"
))




Code
# split strings into characters and pad for alignment
split_and_pad <- function(x, max_len) {
  chars <- unlist(strsplit(as.character(x), ""))
  pad <- rep("_", max_len - nchar(x))
  c(chars, pad)
}

# highlight differing digits in corrected codes
highlight_diff_digits <- function(a, b) {
  # a report set to not codable displays as blank
  if (is.na(b)) {
    return("")
  }

  # a newly coded report has no original digits to compare against
  if (is.na(a)) {
    return(as.character(b))
  }

  # determine maximum length for padding
  max_len <- max(nchar(a), nchar(b))

  # split and pad strings to uniform length
  a_digs <- split_and_pad(a, max_len)
  b_digs <- split_and_pad(b, max_len)

  # define colors for unchanged and changed digits
  clr <- viridis::plasma(20, 1, 0)[seq(4, 12, length.out = 2)]

  # format digits with HTML color coding
  formatted <- paste0(
    mapply(function(init, new) {
      sprintf(
        '<span style="color:%s;">%s</span>',
        ifelse(init == new, clr[1], clr[2]),
        new
      )
    }, a_digs, b_digs),
    collapse = ""
  )

  # return b if formatting fails
  if (is.na(formatted)) {
    return(as.character(b))
  }
  return(formatted)
}




Code
# delivered codes per report, long format, keyed like the coding file
delivered <- main %>%
  dplyr::select(idnummer, dplyr::all_of(paste0("isco08_old", unname(suffixes)))) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(-idnummer, names_to = "column", values_to = "isco08_delivered") %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    suffix = sub("^isco08_old", "", column),
    source = names(suffixes)[match(suffix, unname(suffixes))],
    isco08_delivered = pad4(isco08_delivered)
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(idnummer, source, isco08_delivered)

# every report where the final audited code differs from the delivered code
corrections <- scored %>%
  dplyr::mutate(idnummer = as.integer(idnummer)) %>%
  dplyr::inner_join(delivered, by = c("idnummer", "source")) %>%
  dplyr::filter(dplyr::coalesce(isco08_delivered, "~") != dplyr::coalesce(isco08, "~")) %>%
  dplyr::rowwise() %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    occupation = tolower(dplyr::coalesce(occupation, "")),
    group_original = isco_label(isco08_delivered),
    group_corrected = isco_label(isco08),
    isco08_display = highlight_diff_digits(isco08_delivered, isco08)
  ) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup() %>%
  dplyr::transmute(
    source,
    idnummer,
    occupation,
    isco08_original = dplyr::coalesce(isco08_delivered, ""),
    group_original,
    isco08_corrected = isco08_display,
    group_corrected,
    justification = dplyr::coalesce(justification, "")
  ) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(idnummer)

# split by respondent type and place groups in the global environment
corrections_grouped <- corrections %>% dplyr::group_by(source)
corrections_split <- dplyr::group_split(corrections_grouped, .keep = FALSE)
names(corrections_split) <- dplyr::group_keys(corrections_grouped)$source
list2env(corrections_split, envir = .GlobalEnv)


<environment: R_GlobalEnv>




Code
# per-source comparison of the delivered coding against the final audited coding
correction_summary <- purrr::imap_dfr(suffixes, function(suffix, source_name) {
  old <- as.numeric(main[[paste0("isco08_old", suffix)]])
  new <- as.numeric(main[[paste0("isco08r", suffix)]])
  tibble::tibble(
    source = source_name,
    coded_delivered = sum(!is.na(old)),
    coded_final = sum(!is.na(new)),
    blanked = sum(!is.na(old) & is.na(new)),
    newly_coded = sum(is.na(old) & !is.na(new)),
    changed = sum(!is.na(old) & !is.na(new) & old != new)
  )
}) %>%
  dplyr::bind_rows(dplyr::summarise(
    ., source = "Total", dplyr::across(dplyr::where(is.numeric), sum)
  ))

summary_kable <- correction_summary %>%
  dplyr::mutate(source = stringr::str_to_title(source)) %>%
  dplyr::rename(
    Category = source,
    `Coded (delivered)` = coded_delivered,
    `Coded (final)` = coded_final,
    Blanked = blanked,
    `Newly Coded` = newly_coded,
    Changed = changed
  ) %>%
  as_kable()

kableExtra::row_spec(summary_kable, nrow(correction_summary), bold = TRUE) %>%
  add_style()

# generate the per-source correction table with a descriptive footnote
summarize_isco_corrections <- function(x, source_name = NULL) {
  # use provided source name or infer from variable name
  source_name <- source_name %||% deparse(substitute(x))

  # correction records shown against all report rows for this source
  tot_rows <- sum(scored$source == source_name)
  just_rows <- nrow(x)
  pct_just <- round(just_rows / tot_rows * 100)

  footnote_text <- glue::glue(
    "Correction records: $N={just_rows}$ ({pct_just}% of the {tot_rows} report rows for ",
    "{source_name}). Occupation titles remain in lowercase for consistency with ",
    "reported data. Each row compares the code delivered with the source data against ",
    "the final audited code, with the reasoning recorded during coding review. Red ",
    "digits highlight code modifications, while unchanged digits appear in purple. ",
    "A blank corrected code means the report was set to not codable."
  )

  # format table with styling and footnote
  x %>%
    dplyr::select(-dplyr::any_of("source")) %>%
    stats::setNames(col_names) %>%
    as_kable() %>%
    kableExtra::column_spec(seq_len(length(col_names)), extra_css = "vertical-align: top;") %>%
    footnote() %>%
    add_style()
}

title: ISCO-08 Standardization and Validation
